ogTitle: Ocean Grange vs Officer: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Ocean Grange and Officer.
heading: Ocean Grange vs Officer — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Ocean Grange, Victoria and Officer, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Officer has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Ocean Grange (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Ocean Grange or Officer safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Ocean Grange has a flood risk score of 42/100, while Officer scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Ocean Grange scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Officer scores 39/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Ocean Grange or Officer?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Officer has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
